info.title: NUnit NetCore Console Runner 3.22.0 (Release)
info.description:
Copyright (c) 2022 Charlie Poole, Rob Prouse
Saturday, 28 March 2026 10:23:01
NUNIT3-CONSOLE [inputfiles] [options]

options.name: --test
options.description:
Comma-separated list of NAMES of tests to run or
explore. This option may be repeated.

options.name: --testlist
options.description:
File PATH containing a list of tests to run, one
per line. This option may be repeated.

options.name: --where
options.description:
Test selection EXPRESSION indicating what tests
will be run. See description below.

options.name: --params
options.description:
Deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
Please use --testparam instead.

options.name: --testparam
options.description:
Followed by a key-value pair separated by an
equals sign. Test code can access the value by
name.

options.name: --seed
options.description: Set the random SEED used to generate test cases.

options.name: --workers
options.description:
Specify the NUMBER of worker threads to be used in
running tests. If not specified, defaults to 2
or the number of processors, whichever is
greater.

options.name: --stoponerror
options.description:
Stop run immediately upon any test failure or
error.

options.name: --wait
options.description: Wait for input before closing console window.

options.name: --work
options.description:
PATH of the directory to use for output files. If
not specified, defaults to the current directory.

options.name: --output
options.description: File PATH to contain text output from the tests.

options.name: --result
options.description:
An output SPEC for saving the test results.
This option may be repeated.

options.name: --explore
options.description:
Display or save test info rather than running
tests. Optionally provide an output SPEC for
saving the test info. This option may be
repeated.

options.name: --noresult
options.description: Don't save any test results.

options.name: --labels
options.description:
Specify whether to write test case names to the
output. Values: Off, OnOutputOnly, Before, After,
BeforeAndAfter

options.name: --test-name-format
options.description:
Non-standard naming pattern to use in generating
test names.

options.name: --trace
options.description:
Set internal trace LEVEL.
Values: Off, Error, Warning, Info, Verbose (
Debug)

options.name: --noheader
options.description:
Suppress display of program information at start
of run.

options.name: --nocolor
options.description: Displays console output without color.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this message and exit.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display the header and exit.

options.name: --encoding
options.description:
Specifies the encoding to use for Console standard
output, for example utf-8, ascii, unicode.

options.name: --config
options.description:
NAME of a project configuration to load (e.g.:
Debug).

options.name: --dispose-runners
options.description:
Dispose each test runner after it has finished
running its tests.

options.name: --skipnontestassemblies
options.description:
Skip any non-test assemblies specified, without
error.

options.name: --list-extensions
options.description:
List all extension points and the extensions for
each.

options.name: --extensionDirectory
options.description:
Specifies an additional directory to be examined
for extensions. May be repeated.

options.name: --teamcity
options.description:
Turns on use of TeamCity service messages.
TeamCity engine extension is required.

options.name: --enable
options.description: Enables the specified extension. May be repeated.

options.name: --disable
--include
options.description: describing exactly which tests to use. Examples of usage are:

options.name: --where
options.description:
Care should be taken in combining --where with --test or --testlist.
The test and where specifications are implicitly joined using &&, so
that BOTH sets of criteria must be satisfied in order for a test to run.
See the docs for more information and a full description of the syntax
information and a full description of the syntax.
Several options that specify processing of XML output take
an output specification as a value. A SPEC may take one of
the following forms:

options.name: --OPTION
options.description:
The --result option may use any of the following formats
nunit3 - the native XML format for NUnit 3
nunit2 - legacy XML format used by earlier releases of NUnit
Requires the engine extension NUnitV2ResultWriter.
The --explore option may use any of the following formats:
nunit3 - the native XML format for NUnit 3
cases  - a text file listing the full names of all test cases.
If --explore is used without any specification following, a list of
test cases is output to the writer.
If none of the options {--result, --explore, --noresult} is used,
NUnit saves the results to TestResult.xml in nunit3 format.
Any transforms provided must handle input in the native nunit3 format.
To be able to load NUnit projects, file type .nunit, the engine
extension NUnitProjectLoader is required. For Visual Studio projects
and solutions the engine extension VSProjectLoader is required.
Limitations:
The NetCore Runner is primarily intended for use as a dotnet  tool.
When used in this way, a single assembly is usually being tested and
the assembly must be compatible with execution under the same runtime
as the runner itself, normally .NET 6.0.
Using this runner, the following options are not available. A brief
rationale is given for each option excluded.

options.name: --configFile
options.description: Config of the runner itself is used.

options.name: --process
options.description: Not designed to run out of process.

options.name: --inprocess
options.description: Redundant. We always run in process.

options.name: --domain
options.description: Not applicable to .NET Core.

options.name: --framework
options.description: Runtime of the runner is used.

options.name: --x86
options.description: Bitness of the runner is used.

options.name: --shadowcopy
options.description: Not available.

options.name: --loaduserprofile
options.description: Not available.

options.name: --agents
options.description: No agents are used.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Debug in process directly.

options.name: --pause
options.description: Used for debugging agents.

options.name: --set-principal-policy
options.description: Not available.

options.name: --debug-agent
options.description: No agents are used.
